description: Homeowner who already has 1-2 HVAC quotes in hand and is comparison-shopping. Tighter conversion window than persona1 (active decision mode). 3 emails over 10 days.

subject_lines: Score your HVAC quotes against this 10-item checklist
Your quotes — and the 10 things they should each include
sequence.preheader: A quote missing 3+ of these is telling you something about how the contractor operates.

subject_lines: 5 pricing tricks HVAC contractors use (and how to spot them)
The brand-swap, the labor-only quote, and 3 more tricks to watch for
sequence.preheader: None of these are illegal. All of them benefit the contractor at your expense if you don't catch them.

subject_lines: How to actually pick between HVAC quotes
sequence.preheader: When two quotes are within 5% on price, the right answer is almost never the cheaper one.

personalization_variables.home.sqft: number — square footage from the load calc
personalization_variables.home.tonnage: number — system size in tons (1 ton = 12,000 BTU/hr cooling)
personalization_variables.home.insulation: string — Excellent / Better than avg / Average / Worse than avg / Bad
personalization_variables.location.climate_region: string — Hot / Warm / Mixed / Cool / Cold
personalization_variables.cta_url: string — pre-built Angi URL with utm tags (per-email)

notes: Same personalization data as persona1 — both pull from the load_calculator email_captures row.
tonnage_x_400 is computed inline via {{ home.tonnage | times: 400 | comma }} in day0 item 4.
Footer copy says '3 emails over 10 days' instead of persona1's '4 over 2 weeks' — see partials/footer.liquid.
Resend's {{{unsubscribe_url}}} is wrapped in {% raw %}...{% endraw %} inside footer.liquid so it survives Liquid render and reaches Resend intact.
